title: SP9 side-project dogfood digest
summary: Static dogfood digest for reviewing side-project implementation loops without live GitHub, live .mew state, or core mew imports.

dogfood_rows.label: SP8 archive-index closeout
dogfood_rows.evidence: Archive index mode, README usage, and focused tests were completed before SP9 started.

dogfood_rows.label: SP9 retry after stopped session
dogfood_rows.evidence: The prior session stopped before product edits, so the retry refreshed exact anchors instead of trusting stale notes.

dogfood_rows.label: Dogfood digest output-file coverage
dogfood_rows.evidence: The mode is only complete when stdout and --output behavior are both covered by focused tests.

dogfood_rows.label: Static issue summary rendering
dogfood_rows.evidence: Issue summaries are represented as fixture rows with deterministic links rather than live GitHub queries.

product_progress: SP6 state brief, SP7 bundle, and SP8 archive index provide stable companion surfaces for side-project review.
SP9 adds a dogfood digest lane that makes outcomes, failure classes, rescue_edits counts, and issue follow-up visible in one deterministic markdown report.
blockers: Importing src/mew or reading live .mew state from the experiment remains blocked.
Live GitHub issue lookup is intentionally out of scope for this fixture-driven mode.
m6_16_polish_candidates: Tighten resume guidance when a session stops before product edits.
Make output-file coverage a standard checklist item for new companion-log modes.

side_pj_issues.title: [side-pj] M6.16 rejected-batch retry can accumulate context until timeout
side_pj_issues.summary: Track the rejected-batch retry failure mode where accumulated context can grow until the session times out.

side_pj_issues.title: [side-pj] M6.16 scoped verifier repairs should not require fresh sessions
side_pj_issues.summary: Track scoped verifier repair loops that currently require fresh sessions instead of continuing in place.
